articles.title: An Intelligent Fault Diagnosis Method for General Aviation Aircraft Based on Multi-Fidelity Digital Twin and FMEA Knowledge Enhancement
articles.titleKo: 다중 충실도 디지털 트윈과 FMEA 지식 강화 기반의 일반항공 항공기 지능형 결함 진단 방법
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft faces challenges including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. This paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin, integrating four modules: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, FMEA-driven fault injection, multi-fidelity residual feature extraction, and large language model (LLM)-enhanced interpretable report generation. A digital twin is constructed using the JSBSim six-degree-of-freedom (6-DoF) flight dynamics en
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the practical problem of fault diagnosis in general aviation aircraft, which faces challenges such as scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. The research team developed an artificial intelligence fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin technology. The system consists of four modules: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, FMEA-driven fault injection, multi-fidelity residual feature extraction, and large language model (LLM)-enhanced interpretable report generation. The digital twin was constructed using the JSBSim six-degree-of-freedom flight dynamics engine. The significance of this research is that it can improve aircraft safety by utilizing simulation data even when actual fault data is limited. However, a limitation is that validation with actual flight data may be insufficient.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 일반 항공기의 결함 진단이라는 실제 문제를 해결하기 위해 제안되었습니다. 항공기 결함 진단은 실제 결함 데이터 부족, 다양한 결함 유형, 약한 결함 신호 등의 어려움을 겪고 있습니다. 연구팀은 다중 신뢰도 디지털 트윈(Digital Twin) 기술을 기반으로 한 인공지능 결함 진단 프레임워크를 개발했습니다. 이 시스템은 고충실도 비행 동역학 시뮬레이션, FMEA 기반 결함 주입, 다중 신뢰도 잔여 특징 추출, 대규모 언어모델(LLM) 기반 해석 가능한 보고서 생성이라는 4개 모듈로 구성됩니다. JSBSim이라는 6자유도 비행 동역학 엔진을 사용하여 디지털 트윈을 구축했습니다. 본 연구의 의의는 실제 결함 데이터가 부족한 상황에서도 시뮬레이션 데이터를 활용하여 항공기 안전성을 향상시킬 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만, 실제 비행 데이터와의 검증이 충분하지 않을 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the practical problem of fault diagnosis in general aviation aircraft, which faces challenges such as scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. The research team developed an artificial intelligence fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin technology. The system consists of four modules: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, FMEA-driven fault injection, multi-fidelity residual feature extraction, and large language model (LLM)-enhanced interpretable report generation. The digital twin was constructed using the JSBSim six-degree-of-freedom flight dynamics engine. The significance of this research is that it can improve aircraft safety by utilizing simulation data even when actual fault data is limited. However, a limitation is that validation with actual flight data may be insufficient.

##
keyPoints: **다중 신뢰도 디지털 트윈 기술** / Multi-fidelity digital twin technology for fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft
**FMEA 기반 결함 주입 시스템** / FMEA-driven fault injection module to generate diverse fault scenarios and enhance knowledge base
**LLM을 활용한 해석 가능성** / Large language model integration for generating interpretable fault diagnosis reports that practitioners can understand
**시뮬레이션 기반 대체 솔루션** / Simulation-based approach using JSBSim 6-DoF engine to overcome scarce real fault data limitation

articles.title: PExA: Parallel Exploration Agent for Complex Text-to-SQL
articles.titleKo: PExA: 복잡한 Text-to-SQL을 위한 병렬 탐색 에이전트
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22934v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agents for text-to-SQL often struggle with latency-performance trade-off, where performance improvements come at the cost of latency or vice versa. We reformulate text-to-SQL generation within the lens of software test coverage where the original query is prepared with a suite of test cases with simpler, atomic SQLs that are executed in parallel and together ensure semantic coverage of the original query. After iterating on test case coverage, the final SQL is generated only when enough information is gathered, leveraging the explored t
articles.aiSummary: This research addresses the latency-performance trade-off faced by LLM-based text-to-SQL conversion agents by proposing a novel approach inspired by software test coverage principles. The research team decomposes complex original queries into simpler, atomic SQL test cases that are executed in parallel to collectively ensure semantic coverage of the original query. The proposed PExA (Parallel Exploration Agent) system iteratively refines test case coverage and generates the final SQL only after sufficient information is gathered through parallel exploration. By leveraging parallel processing instead of sequential approaches, the method aims to reduce latency while maintaining or improving performance. The system reformulates the text-to-SQL generation problem, treating it as a coverage problem rather than a direct translation task. A key limitation is that specific performance metrics and benchmark results are not yet provided in the abstract, making it difficult to fully evaluate the practical effectiveness of the proposed approach.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 본 연구는 LLM 기반 텍스트-SQL 변환 에이전트가 직면한 지연시간과 성능 간의 상충 관계를 해결하기 위한 새로운 접근법을 제시합니다. 연구팀은 소프트웨어 테스트 커버리지 개념을 텍스트-SQL 생성에 적용하여, 복잡한 원본 쿼리를 더 간단한 원자적(atomic) SQL 테스트 케이스들로 분해하고 이들을 병렬로 실행하는 방식을 제안했습니다. 이 방법은 여러 개의 간단한 SQL 쿼리를 동시에 처리함으로써 전체 원본 쿼리의 의미론적 범위를 포괄하도록 설계되었습니다. PExA(Parallel Exploration Agent)라 명명된 이 방식은 충분한 정보가 수집될 때까지 테스트 케이스 커버리지를 반복적으로 개선한 후 최종 SQL을 생성합니다. 이를 통해 기존의 순차적 처리 방식에서 벗어나 병렬 처리의 이점을 활용하여 지연시간을 줄이면서도 성능을 유지할 수 있습니다. 본 연구의 한계는 아직 구체적인 성능 수치와 벤치마크 결과가 초록 내용에 제시되지 않아 실제 효과를 완전히 평가하기 어렵다는 점입니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: This research addresses the latency-performance trade-off faced by LLM-based text-to-SQL conversion agents by proposing a novel approach inspired by software test coverage principles. The research team decomposes complex original queries into simpler, atomic SQL test cases that are executed in parallel to collectively ensure semantic coverage of the original query. The proposed PExA (Parallel Exploration Agent) system iteratively refines test case coverage and generates the final SQL only after sufficient information is gathered through parallel exploration. By leveraging parallel processing instead of sequential approaches, the method aims to reduce latency while maintaining or improving performance. The system reformulates the text-to-SQL generation problem, treating it as a coverage problem rather than a direct translation task. A key limitation is that specific performance metrics and benchmark results are not yet provided in the abstract, making it difficult to fully evaluate the practical effectiveness of the proposed approach.
keyPoints: **지연시간-성능 상충 해결** / Latency-Performance Trade-off Resolution
SQL 에이전트의 핵심 문제인 지연시간과 성능 간 상충관계를 병렬 처리를 통해 해결하려는 시도
**병렬 탐색 에이전트(PExA) 방식** / Parallel Exploration Agent Approach
**소프트웨어 테스트 커버리지 원리 적용** / Software Test Coverage Principle Application

articles.title: The Power of Power Law: Asymmetry Enables Compositional Reasoning
articles.titleKo: 거듭제곱 법칙의 힘: 비대칭성이 구성적 추론을 가능하게 하다
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22951v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, with most knowledge and skills appearing at very low frequency. While a common intuition suggests that reweighting or curating data towards a uniform distribution may help models better learn these long-tail skills, we find a counterintuitive result: across a wide range of compositional reasoning tasks, such as state tracking and multi-step arithmetic, training under power-law distributions consistently outperforms training under uniform distributions. To understand this advantage, we introd
articles.aiSummary: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, where most knowledge and skills appear at very low frequency. While conventional wisdom suggests that reweighting data toward uniform distribution would help models learn these rare long-tail skills better, this research reveals a counterintuitive finding: across various compositional reasoning tasks such as state tracking and multi-step arithmetic, models trained on power-law distributions consistently outperform those trained on uniform distributions. The researchers introduced a new analytical framework to explain this unexpected advantage, challenging existing assumptions about how AI models learn from data. This discovery has significant implications for data curation strategies in large language model training. However, the study focuses on specific types of reasoning tasks, so validation across other learning domains remains necessary.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 자연언어 데이터는 멱법칙(power-law) 분포를 따르며, 대부분의 지식과 기술이 매우 낮은 빈도로 나타난다. 일반적으로 데이터를 균등 분포로 조정하면 모델이 드물게 나타나는 기술을 더 잘 학습할 것이라고 예상되지만, 이 연구는 놀랍게도 상태 추적(state tracking)과 다단계 산술 같은 다양한 조합 추론 과제에서 멱법칙 분포로 훈련한 모델이 균등 분포로 훈련한 모델보다 일관되게 더 우수한 성능을 보인다는 것을 발견했다. 연구진은 이러한 이점을 설명하기 위해 새로운 분석 방법을 도입했으며, 이는 AI 모델의 데이터 학습 방식에 대한 기존의 통념에 도전한다. 이 발견은 대규모 언어모델 학습 시 데이터 균형 조정 전략에 중요한 함의를 제공한다. 다만 연구는 특정 유형의 추론 과제에 중점을 두고 있어 다른 종류의 학습 과제에 대한 검증이 필요하다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, where most knowledge and skills appear at very low frequency. While conventional wisdom suggests that reweighting data toward uniform distribution would help models learn these rare long-tail skills better, this research reveals a counterintuitive finding: across various compositional reasoning tasks such as state tracking and multi-step arithmetic, models trained on power-law distributions consistently outperform those trained on uniform distributions. The researchers introduced a new analytical framework to explain this unexpected advantage, challenging existing assumptions about how AI models learn from data. This discovery has significant implications for data curation strategies in large language model training. However, the study focuses on specific types of reasoning tasks, so validation across other learning domains remains necessary.
keyPoints: **멱법칙 분포의 우월성** / Power-law distributions outperform uniform distributions in compositional reasoning tasks, contradicting conventional intuition about data rebalancing.
**조합 추론 과제에서의 일관된 성능** / Consistent performance improvement observed across diverse compositional reasoning tasks including state tracking and multi-step arithmetic.
**기존 통념에 대한 도전** / The research challenges the prevailing assumption that uniform data distribution is optimal for learning rare or low-frequency skills.
**모델 학습 메커니즘의 새로운 이해** / Introduction of new analytical framework provides insights into how neural networks leverage natural data distributions for better reasoning and generalization capabilities.

articles.title: On the Existence of an Inverse Solution for Preference-Based Reductions in Argumentation
articles.titleKo: 논증에서 선호도 기반 축약에 대한 역해의 존재성에 관하여
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22958v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs) extend Dung's approach to abstract argumentation (AAFs) by encoding preferences over arguments. Such preferences control the transformation of attacks into defeats, and different approaches to doing so result in different reductions from a PAF to an AAF. In this paper we consider a PAF inverse problem which takes an argumentation graph, a labelling and a semantics as an input, and outputs a ``yes" or ``no" as to whether there is a preference relation between the arguments which can yield the desire
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses an inverse problem in preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs), which extend Dung's abstract argumentation frameworks (AAFs) by encoding preferences among arguments. PAFs transform attack relations into defeats based on such preferences, and different transformation approaches yield different reductions. The research takes an argumentation graph, labeling, and semantics as input to determine whether a preference relation exists that can produce desired outcomes. The team developed a mathematical methodology to analyze the existence of inverse solutions under specific conditions, contributing to the theoretical foundation of argumentation-based reasoning systems. These findings can be applied to AI-driven automated decision-making and dispute resolution mechanisms. However, the study focuses primarily on theoretical analysis, requiring further investigation into computational complexity and scalability in practical applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 논증 이론(argumentation theory)에서 선호도 기반의 논증 체계(PAF)에 관한 역문제를 다룬다. 기존의 추상적 논증 체계(AAF)를 확장한 PAF는 논증들 간의 선호도를 인코딩하여 공격 관계를 '격퇴'로 변환하는데, 이러한 변환 방식에 따라 서로 다른 축약이 발생한다. 본 연구에서는 논증 그래프, 라벨링, 의미론을 입력으로 받아 특정 선호 관계가 원하는 결과를 도출할 수 있는지를 판단하는 역문제를 제시한다. 연구팀은 선호도 기반 축약의 역해를 수학적으로 분석하여, 특정 조건 하에서 해가 존재하는지를 판정하는 방법론을 개발했다. 이러한 발견은 인공지능의 논증 기반 추론 시스템 설계에 활용될 수 있으며, 자동화된 의사결정 및 분쟁 해결 메커니즘 개발에 기여할 수 있다. 다만 본 연구는 이론적 분석에 중점을 두고 있어 실제 적용 시나리오에서의 계산복잡도와 확장성에 대한 추가 검토가 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study addresses an inverse problem in preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs), which extend Dung's abstract argumentation frameworks (AAFs) by encoding preferences among arguments. PAFs transform attack relations into defeats based on such preferences, and different transformation approaches yield different reductions. The research takes an argumentation graph, labeling, and semantics as input to determine whether a preference relation exists that can produce desired outcomes. The team developed a mathematical methodology to analyze the existence of inverse solutions under specific conditions, contributing to the theoretical foundation of argumentation-based reasoning systems. These findings can be applied to AI-driven automated decision-making and dispute resolution mechanisms. However, the study focuses primarily on theoretical analysis, requiring further investigation into computational complexity and scalability in practical applications.

##
keyPoints: **선호도 기반 논증 체계의 역문제 제시** / Introduction of inverse problem for preference-based argumentation frameworks
**논증 그래프에서 선호 관계 존재 판정 가능성** / Determination of whether preference relations can yield desired argumentation outcomes
**AI 기반 추론 및 의사결정 시스템 설계의 이론적 토대 제공** / Provides theoretical foundation for designing AI-driven reasoning and decision-making systems
**실제 응용 시 계산복잡도와 확장성 평가 필요** / Further evaluation of computational complexity and scalability required for practical applications

articles.title: GCA-BULF: A Bottom-Up Framework for Short-Term Load Forecasting Using Grouped Critical Appliances
articles.titleKo: GCA-BULF: 그룹화된 주요 전기기기를 이용한 단기 부하 예측을 위한 상향식 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24766v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the rise of time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing, energy consumers are encouraged to adopt peak-shifting strategies by automatically controlling high-power appliances. These help lower energy costs while enhancing the power grid's stability. To support such energy management with high resilience and responsiveness, reliable short-term load forecasting (STLF) plays a critical role. STLF predicts electricity consumption over time horizons ranging from minutes to days, using historical data, temporal patterns, and contextual factors. Trad
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the growing need for short-term load forecasting (STLF) as time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing encourage consumers to shift peak consumption through automated control of high-power appliances. STLF predicts electricity consumption over minutes-to-days horizons using historical data, temporal patterns, and contextual factors, which is essential for resilient energy management and grid stability. The researchers propose GCA-BULF, a bottom-up framework that groups critical appliances (refrigerators, washing machines, etc.) and predicts household power consumption by aggregating individual device usage patterns rather than forecasting total demand directly. This approach achieves higher accuracy compared to traditional top-down methods by capturing granular consumption behaviors of specific appliances. The framework can significantly improve peak load management and grid stability while supporting cost reduction for energy consumers. However, limitations include variability in appliance configurations across different households and unpredictability of user behavior patterns that may hinder real-world implementation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 시간대별 전기요금제와 계층별 요금제의 확대에 따라 가정과 기업이 피크 시간대 전력 사용을 줄이기 위해 고전력 가전제품을 자동으로 제어해야 하는 필요성에서 출발했습니다. 단기 부하 예측(STLF)은 수분에서 수일 범위의 전력 소비를 역사 데이터와 시간적 패턴을 이용해 예측하는 기술로, 효율적인 에너지 관리에 필수적입니다. 연구팀은 GCA-BULF라는 새로운 프레임워크를 제안했으며, 이는 주요 가전제품(냉장고, 세탁기 등)을 그룹화하여 상향식 방식으로 전력 소비를 예측합니다. 이 방식은 개별 기기의 사용 패턴을 파악하여 전체 전력 수요를 더 정확하게 예측할 수 있습니다. 기존의 하향식 방법(전체 소비량을 직접 예측)보다 더 높은 정확도를 달성할 수 있으며, 피크 전력 관리와 그리드 안정성 향상에 기여합니다. 다만, 실제 가정마다 가전제품 구성이 다르고 사용자 행동이 불규칙할 수 있다는 점이 실무 적용의 한계입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the growing need for short-term load forecasting (STLF) as time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing encourage consumers to shift peak consumption through automated control of high-power appliances. STLF predicts electricity consumption over minutes-to-days horizons using historical data, temporal patterns, and contextual factors, which is essential for resilient energy management and grid stability. The researchers propose GCA-BULF, a bottom-up framework that groups critical appliances (refrigerators, washing machines, etc.) and predicts household power consumption by aggregating individual device usage patterns rather than forecasting total demand directly. This approach achieves higher accuracy compared to traditional top-down methods by capturing granular consumption behaviors of specific appliances. The framework can significantly improve peak load management and grid stability while supporting cost reduction for energy consumers. However, limitations include variability in appliance configurations across different households and unpredictability of user behavior patterns that may hinder real-world implementation.

##
keyPoints: **상향식 예측 방식 도입** / Bottom-up forecasting approach: 개별 가전제품의 사용 패턴을 먼저 예측한 후 합산하여 전체 전력 소비량을 추정하는 혁신적 방법론 제시
**주요 가전제품 그룹화(GCA)** / Grouped Critical Appliances: 냉장고, 에어컨, 세탁기 등 고전력 가전기기를 그룹화하여 예측 정확도 향상
**에너지 비용 절감과 그리드 안정성** / Cost reduction and grid stability: 피크 타임 전력 사용 자동 제어를 통해 에너지 비용을 낮추고 전력망의 안정성을 동시에 개선
**실무 적용의 한계** / Real-world implementation challenges: 가정별 다양한 가전제품 구성과 예측 불가능한 사용자 행동 패턴이 실제 도입 시 정확도에 영향을 미칠 수 있음

articles.title: Automated detection of pediatric congenital heart disease from phonocardiograms using deep and handcrafted feature fusion
articles.titleKo: 딥러닝과 수작업 특징 융합을 이용한 심음도에서의 소아 선천성 심장질환 자동 검출
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24767v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Congenital heart disease (CHD) is the most common type of birth defect, impacting about 1% of live births worldwide. Echocardiography, the gold-standard diagnostic method, is costly and inaccessible in low-resource settings. Diagnosis is delayed due to limited skilled experts, whose ability to interpret pathological patterns varies significantly, causing inter- and intra-clinician variability. Therefore, we present a new method for a more accessible diagnostic modality, the digital stethoscope, to detect CHDs. Our method is based on deep feature
articles.aiSummary:
Congenital heart disease (CHD) is the most common birth defect, affecting approximately 1% of live births worldwide, but the gold-standard diagnostic method (echocardiography) is costly and inaccessible in low-resource settings. This study proposes a new approach using artificial intelligence to automatically analyze heart sounds recorded with digital stethoscopes to detect CHD, potentially addressing diagnostic delays caused by limited skilled experts and high inter-clinician variability. The researchers employed a hybrid method combining deep learning and handcrafted feature extraction techniques to identify pathological patterns in audio signals. This approach can detect cardiac abnormalities with high accuracy without relying on echocardiography, thereby improving accessibility to early diagnosis in resource-limited regions. The significance of the research lies in providing a practical diagnostic pathway for children in low-income areas to receive timely cardiac screening. However, the study has limitations including the need for validation in real clinical settings and testing across diverse populations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
선천성 심장병(CHD)은 전 세계 신생아의 약 1%에 영향을 미치는 가장 흔한 선천적 결손이지만, 표준 진단법인 초음파검사는 비용이 높고 저소득 국가에서 접근이 어렵다. 이 연구는 디지털 청진기로 기록한 심음(음성 신호)을 인공지능으로 자동 분석하여 선천성 심장병을 진단하는 새로운 방법을 제안했다. 연구팀은 심층 학습(딥러닝)과 수작업 특성 추출 두 가지 기법을 결합한 하이브리드 방식을 사용하여 음성 신호에서 특징을 추출했다. 이 방법은 초음파에 의존하지 않으면서도 높은 정확도로 심장 질환을 감지할 수 있어, 의료 전문가의 부족이나 진단 편차 문제를 완화할 수 있다. 연구의 의의는 저소득 지역의 아이들이 더 쉽게 심장병을 조기 진단받을 수 있는 경로를 제시한다는 점이다. 다만 실제 임상 환경에서의 검증과 다양한 인구집단에 대한 추가 테스트가 필요하다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Congenital heart disease (CHD) is the most common birth defect, affecting approximately 1% of live births worldwide, but the gold-standard diagnostic method (echocardiography) is costly and inaccessible in low-resource settings. This study proposes a new approach using artificial intelligence to automatically analyze heart sounds recorded with digital stethoscopes to detect CHD, potentially addressing diagnostic delays caused by limited skilled experts and high inter-clinician variability. The researchers employed a hybrid method combining deep learning and handcrafted feature extraction techniques to identify pathological patterns in audio signals. This approach can detect cardiac abnormalities with high accuracy without relying on echocardiography, thereby improving accessibility to early diagnosis in resource-limited regions. The significance of the research lies in providing a practical diagnostic pathway for children in low-income areas to receive timely cardiac screening. However, the study has limitations including the need for validation in real clinical settings and testing across diverse populations.

##
keyPoints: **질병의 범위와 진단 격차** / Global burden of CHD (1% of live births) and diagnostic accessibility gap in low-resource settings
**혁신적 기술 접근** / Hybrid AI method combining deep learning and handcrafted features for automated phonocardiogram analysis using digital stethoscopes
**임상적 의의** / Potential to reduce diagnostic delays and clinician variability while enabling early detection in underserved populations
**필요한 추가 연구** / Validation in diverse clinical settings and across different populations required for real-world clinical implementation

articles.title: Comparative Study of Bending Analysis using Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Numerical Dynamic Deflection in Perforated nanobeam
articles.titleKo: 천공된 나노보의 물리정보신경망을 이용한 굽힘해석과 수치동적처짐의 비교연구
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24768v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this chapter, we investigate the bending behavior of a perforated nanobeam subjected to sinusoidal loading using an efficient and computationally robust Physics-Informed Functional Link Constrained Framework with Domain Mapping (DFL-TFC) method. Our aim is to determine the relationship between static bending response and dynamic deflection of a perforated nanobeam for various perforation cases. The static bending is obtained using the FL-TFC with Domain mapped method, whereas dynamic deflection is determined using the Galerkin method. The prop
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates the bending behavior of perforated nanobeams subjected to sinusoidal loading using a novel Physics-Informed Neural Network approach called DFL-TFC (Physics-Informed Functional Link Constrained Framework with Domain Mapping). The researchers compared static bending responses and dynamic deflections across various perforation configurations in nanobeams using two computational methods: FL-TFC with domain mapping for static analysis and the Galerkin method for dynamic deflection analysis. The significance of this research lies in providing an efficient, computationally robust AI-based solution that can replace traditional costly numerical methods in structural analysis. The methodology can be applied to nanobeam and microstructure design optimization with the ability to analyze diverse perforation patterns. However, the study is limited to sinusoidal loading conditions, and validation under complex real-world loading scenarios remains necessary. This work bridges artificial intelligence and materials science, potentially accelerating computational design processes in nanotechnology and microelectromechanical systems (MEMS).

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 물리 정보 신경망(Physics-Informed Neural Networks)을 활용하여 구멍이 뚫린 나노빔의 굽힘 거동을 분석하는 새로운 방법을 제시합니다. 연구팀은 정현파 하중을 받는 천공 나노빔의 정적 굽힘 반응과 동적 처짐을 비교하기 위해 DFL-TFC(Domain Mapping을 적용한 물리 정보 함수 연결 제약 프레임워크) 방법을 개발했습니다. 정적 굽힘은 FL-TFC 도메인 매핑 방법으로, 동적 처짐은 갈레르킨 방법으로 각각 계산되었습니다. 이 연구의 의의는 기존의 계산 비용이 높은 수치해석 방법을 대체할 수 있는 효율적이고 강건한 AI 기반 솔루션을 제공한다는 점입니다. 나노소재 및 마이크로기계 분야의 구조물 설계와 최적화에 활용될 수 있으며, 다양한 천공 패턴에 대한 광범위한 분석이 가능합니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 하중 조건(정현파 하중)에 제한되어 있으며, 실제 복잡한 하중 조건에 대한 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigates the bending behavior of perforated nanobeams subjected to sinusoidal loading using a novel Physics-Informed Neural Network approach called DFL-TFC (Physics-Informed Functional Link Constrained Framework with Domain Mapping). The researchers compared static bending responses and dynamic deflections across various perforation configurations in nanobeams using two computational methods: FL-TFC with domain mapping for static analysis and the Galerkin method for dynamic deflection analysis. The significance of this research lies in providing an efficient, computationally robust AI-based solution that can replace traditional costly numerical methods in structural analysis. The methodology can be applied to nanobeam and microstructure design optimization with the ability to analyze diverse perforation patterns. However, the study is limited to sinusoidal loading conditions, and validation under complex real-world loading scenarios remains necessary. This work bridges artificial intelligence and materials science, potentially accelerating computational design processes in nanotechnology and microelectromechanical systems (MEMS).

##
keyPoints: **AI 기반 혁신적 해석 방법** / Novel AI-Based Numerical Analysis Method
TFC 방법으로 기존의 시간 소모적이고 비용이 높은 수치해석을 대체할 수 있는 효율적인 솔루션 제시
**정적-동적 거동 비교 분석** / Static-Dynamic Behavior Comparative Analysis
**나노기술 응용 가능성** / Applications in Nanotechnology and MEMS

articles.title: Liquid Neural Network Models for Natural Gas Spot Price Time-Series Forecasting
articles.titleKo: 천연가스 현물가격 시계열 예측을 위한 액체 신경망 모델
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24788v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural gas is undoubtedly an essential component of the global energy system. Accurate short-term forecasting of natural gas price is challenging due to pronounced volatility driven by seasonal demand patterns, geopolitical developments, and shifting macroeconomic conditions. The nonlinear dynamics and frequent regime changes can limit the effectiveness of traditional time-series models. In this study, we explore the use of Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs) for short-horizon forecasting of the Henry Hub spot price, a primary benchmark for pricing. L
articles.aiSummary:
This study introduces Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs), an advanced AI model, to address the challenges of natural gas price forecasting. Natural gas is a critical component of the global energy system, but accurate short-term prediction is extremely difficult due to severe price volatility caused by seasonal demand changes, geopolitical events, and macroeconomic fluctuations. The research applies LNNs to forecast the Henry Hub spot price, a major benchmark for U.S. natural gas pricing. LNNs can effectively learn nonlinear dynamic systems that change over time, overcoming the limitations of traditional time-series models. The significance of this research lies in successfully applying cutting-edge AI technology to a financially unpredictable field, potentially developing more accurate forecasting models. However, the study is limited to a single commodity (natural gas) and may not capture all variables present in real trading environments.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 천연가스 가격 예측의 어려움을 해결하기 위해 액체 신경망(Liquid Neural Networks, LNNs)이라는 새로운 AI 모델을 도입했습니다. 천연가스는 전 세계 에너지 시스템의 핵심 요소이지만, 계절 수요 변화, 지정학적 사건, 거시경제 변화로 인한 심한 가격 변동성으로 인해 정확한 단기 예측이 매우 어렵습니다. 이 연구는 미국 천연가스의 주요 가격 지표인 헨리 허브(Henry Hub) 현물 가격을 예측하기 위해 액체 신경망 모델을 적용했습니다. 액체 신경망은 시간에 따라 변하는 비선형 동역학 시스템을 효과적으로 학습할 수 있어 기존 시계열 모델의 한계를 극복할 수 있습니다. 본 연구의 주요 의의는 금융 시장의 예측 불가능성이 높은 분야에 첨단 AI 기술을 적용하여 더 정확한 예측 모델을 개발했다는 점입니다. 다만 이 연구는 단일 상품(천연가스)에 제한되어 있으며, 실제 거래 환경의 다양한 변수를 모두 반영하지 못할 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study introduces Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs), an advanced AI model, to address the challenges of natural gas price forecasting. Natural gas is a critical component of the global energy system, but accurate short-term prediction is extremely difficult due to severe price volatility caused by seasonal demand changes, geopolitical events, and macroeconomic fluctuations. The research applies LNNs to forecast the Henry Hub spot price, a major benchmark for U.S. natural gas pricing. LNNs can effectively learn nonlinear dynamic systems that change over time, overcoming the limitations of traditional time-series models. The significance of this research lies in successfully applying cutting-edge AI technology to a financially unpredictable field, potentially developing more accurate forecasting models. However, the study is limited to a single commodity (natural gas) and may not capture all variables present in real trading environments.

##
keyPoints: **액체 신경망(LNN) 도입** / Application of Liquid Neural Networks as an advanced forecasting model for volatile financial time series data
**비선형 동역학 학습 능력** / Superior ability to learn nonlinear dynamics and regime changes compared to traditional statistical models, addressing the core challenge of natural gas price volatility
**헨리 허브 현물 가격 예측** / Focus on Henry Hub spot price forecasting, which serves as the primary benchmark for natural gas pricing in global markets
**단일 상품 제약과 실무 적용의 간극** / Limited to natural gas forecasting and potential gap between model predictions and real-world trading conditions with multiple confounding variables

articles.title: Neuronal electricality founded in murburn-thermodynamic principles: 1. Background and basic theoretical formulation
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24772v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Trans-membrane gradients and fluxes of cations (H+, Na+, K+, etc.) were deemed to be the rationale of electrical activities of aerobic cells/organelles, as per classical perceptions. Murburn concept (an umbrella of theorization based in stochastic redox processes) has afforded novel models for various metabolic, bioenergetic and electrophysiological outcomes. Herein, the foundational mechanistic formalisms for the electrical activities of neurons that lead signal relay along the axonal length are provided. Electron Holding potential (EHP), a dime

articles.title: One-shot emergency psychiatric triage across 15 frontier AI chatbots
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.25415v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI chatbots are increasingly used for health advice, but their performance in psychiatric triage remains undercharacterized. Psychiatric triage is particularly challenging because urgency must often be inferred from thoughts, behavior, and context rather than from objective findings. We evaluated the performance of 15 frontier AI chatbots on psychiatric triage from realistic single-message disclosures using 112 clinical vignettes, each paired with 1 of 4 original benchmark triage labels: A, routine; B, assessment within 1 week; C, assessment with

articles.title: A geometry aware framework enhances noninvasive mapping of whole human brain dynamics
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.25592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Non-invasive electrophysiology lacks methods that accurately reconstruct whole-brain spatiotemporal dynamics while incorporating individual cortical geometry, leaving current electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography source imaging limited by simplistic or biologically implausible priors. Here, we show that embedding participant-specific Geometric Basis Functions (GBFs), eigenmodes derived from each individual's cortical surface, provides a powerful anatomic constraint that resolves the inverse problem and improves reconstruction fidelity

articles.title: Independent-Component-Based Encoding Models of Brain Activity During Story Comprehension
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.24942v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Encoding models provide a powerful framework for linking continuous stimulus features to neural activity; however, traditional voxelwise approaches are limited by measurement noise, inter-subject variability, and redundancy arising from spatially correlated voxels encoding overlapping neural signals. Here, we propose an independent component (IC)-based encoding framework that dissociates stimulus-driven and noise-driven signals in fMRI data. We decompose continuous fMRI data from naturalistic story listening into ICs using one subset of the dat

articles.title: MIT scientists turn chaotic laser light into powerful brain imaging tool
articles.summary: Scientists at MIT discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused beam instead of scattering—if the conditions are just right. This “pencil beam” enabled them to image the blood-brain barrier in 3D at speeds 25 times faster than existing techniques. The method also lets researchers watch how drugs move into brain cells in real time. It could dramatically accelerate the development of treatments for neurological diseases.

articles.title: Your dreams aren’t random. Here’s what’s really happening
articles.summary: Dreams are more structured than they seem, shaped by both personal traits and real-world experiences. Researchers found that the brain doesn’t just replay daily life—it reshapes it into imaginative, sometimes surreal scenarios. People who mind-wander more tend to have fragmented dreams, while those who value dreams experience richer ones. Even major events like the pandemic changed dream content, making it more emotional and restrictive.

articles.title: This hidden kind of stress may be damaging your memory as you age
articles.summary: A new study reveals that internalizing stress—especially feelings of hopelessness—may significantly speed up memory decline in older Chinese Americans. Surprisingly, factors like community support didn’t show the same impact. Researchers say cultural pressures and stereotypes may cause emotional struggles to go unnoticed and untreated. The findings suggest that targeted, culturally sensitive stress relief could play a powerful role in preserving cognitive health.

articles.title: Scientists may have found the brain’s switch for chronic pain
articles.summary: Deep within the brain, scientists have uncovered a hidden “switch” that may decide whether pain fades away—or lingers for months or even years. Researchers found that a small, little-known region called the caudal granular insular cortex (CGIC) acts like a command center, telling the body to keep pain signals alive long after an injury has healed. In animal studies, shutting down this pathway not only prevented chronic pain from forming but could even erase it once it had taken hold.

articles.title: MolClaw: An Autonomous Agent with Hierarchical Skills for Drug Molecule Evaluation, Screening, and Optimization
articles.titleKo: MolClaw: 약물 분자 평가, 스크리닝 및 최적화를 위한 계층적 기술을 갖춘 자율 에이전트
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computational drug discovery, particularly the complex workflows of drug molecule screening and optimization, requires orchestrating dozens of specialized tools in multi-step workflows, yet current AI agents struggle to maintain robust performance and consistently underperform in these high-complexity scenarios. Here we present MolClaw, an autonomous agent that leads drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization. It unifies over 30 specialized domain resources through a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture (70 skills in total) that
articles.aiSummary:
Computational drug discovery involves complex workflows requiring the orchestration of dozens of specialized tools for drug molecule screening and optimization, but current AI agents struggle to maintain consistent performance in these high-complexity scenarios. Researchers developed MolClaw, an autonomous agent designed to perform drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization automatically. MolClaw integrates over 30 specialized domain resources through a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture comprising 70 total skills, enabling effective management of intricate drug development workflows. This hierarchical structure significantly enhances the performance consistency and reliability of the AI agent compared to existing approaches. The research demonstrates that this architectural innovation can substantially improve the efficiency of computational drug discovery. However, validation in real clinical settings and further research on diverse molecular types remains necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
신약 개발 과정에서 분자 평가, 스크리닝, 최적화 같은 복잡한 작업들은 수십 개의 전문 도구를 단계별로 활용해야 하는데, 기존 AI 에이전트들은 이러한 고복잡도 작업에서 성능 저하 문제를 겪었다. 연구팀은 이를 해결하기 위해 MolClaw라는 자동 AI 에이전트를 개발했다. MolClaw는 30개 이상의 전문 도메인 자원을 3단계 계층적 기술 아키텍처(총 70개 기술)로 통합하여, 약물 분자의 평가와 스크리닝, 최적화를 자동으로 수행할 수 있다. 이 계층적 구조는 복잡한 약물 개발 워크플로우를 효과적으로 관리하면서 AI 에이전트의 성능 일관성을 높인다. 본 연구는 기존 에이전트의 한계를 극복하고 계산 기반 신약 개발의 효율성을 크게 향상시킬 수 있음을 시사한다. 다만 실제 임상 환경에서의 검증과 다양한 분자 유형에 대한 추가 연구가 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Computational drug discovery involves complex workflows requiring the orchestration of dozens of specialized tools for drug molecule screening and optimization, but current AI agents struggle to maintain consistent performance in these high-complexity scenarios. Researchers developed MolClaw, an autonomous agent designed to perform drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization automatically. MolClaw integrates over 30 specialized domain resources through a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture comprising 70 total skills, enabling effective management of intricate drug development workflows. This hierarchical structure significantly enhances the performance consistency and reliability of the AI agent compared to existing approaches. The research demonstrates that this architectural innovation can substantially improve the efficiency of computational drug discovery. However, validation in real clinical settings and further research on diverse molecular types remains necessary.

##
keyPoints: **계층적 AI 아키텍처의 중요성** / Importance of Hierarchical AI Architecture: 3단계 계층적 기술 구조(70개 기술)를 통해 복잡한 약물 개발 프로세스를 체계적으로 관리
**통합 도메인 자원의 활용** / Integration of Specialized Domain Resources: 30개 이상의 전문 도메인 자원을 통합하여 약물 분자 평가, 스크리닝, 최적화 작업 자동화
**기존 AI 에이전트의 성능 향상** / Performance Improvement Over Existing Agents: 고복잡도 시나리오에서 일관된 성능 유지로 기존 에이전트의 성능 저하 문제 해결
**신약 개발 혁신의 가능성** / Potential for Drug Discovery Innovation: 자동화된 약물 최적화 프로세스는 신약 개발 시간 단축 및 비용 절감의 가능성을 제시

articles.title: Read the Paper, Write the Code: Agentic Reproduction of Social-Science Results
articles.titleKo: 논문을 읽고 코드를 작성하다: 사회과학 결과의 에이전트 기반 재현
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21965v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has used LLM agents to reproduce empirical social science results with access to both the data and code. We broaden this scope by asking: Can they reproduce results given only a paper's methods description and original data? We develop an agentic reproduction system that extracts structured methods descriptions from papers, runs reimplementations under strict information isolation -- agents never see the original code, results, or paper -- and enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs to the original results.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates whether large language model (LLM)-based AI agents can reproduce social science research results using only paper method descriptions and original data, without access to the original code. While previous work provided agents with both code and results, this research team developed a novel system testing reproduction capability under strict information isolation. The researchers automatically extract structured method descriptions from papers and have agents reimplemented analyses in a controlled environment where they cannot see original code, results, or papers. The system enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs against original results. This approach demonstrates potential to automate scientific transparency and reproducibility, thereby enhancing academic credibility. However, limitations include unclear success rates across different study types, handling of complex statistical methodologies, and the impact of ambiguity in natural language descriptions on reproduction accuracy.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모 언어모델(LLM) 기반 AI 에이전트가 사회과학 논문의 방법론 설명과 원본 데이터만으로 연구 결과를 재현할 수 있는지를 탐구합니다. 기존 연구들은 에이전트에게 원본 코드와 결과를 제공했지만, 이 연구팀은 이러한 정보 없이도 재현이 가능한지 테스트하는 새로운 시스템을 개발했습니다. 연구자들은 논문에서 자동으로 구조화된 방법론 설명을 추출하고, 에이전트가 원본 코드나 결과를 보지 못하도록 엄격하게 격리된 환경에서 재구현을 수행하도록 설계했습니다. 이 시스템은 재현된 결과를 셀(cell) 단위로 원본 결과와 결정적으로 비교할 수 있습니다. 이러한 접근법은 과학적 투명성과 재현 가능성을 자동화하여 학문적 신뢰성을 높일 수 있는 잠재력을 보여줍니다. 다만 연구에서는 재현 성공률, 복잡한 통계 방법론 처리, 그리고 자연어 설명의 모호성이 얼마나 영향을 미치는지 구체적으로 밝혀야 한다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigates whether large language model (LLM)-based AI agents can reproduce social science research results using only paper method descriptions and original data, without access to the original code. While previous work provided agents with both code and results, this research team developed a novel system testing reproduction capability under strict information isolation. The researchers automatically extract structured method descriptions from papers and have agents reimplemented analyses in a controlled environment where they cannot see original code, results, or papers. The system enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs against original results. This approach demonstrates potential to automate scientific transparency and reproducibility, thereby enhancing academic credibility. However, limitations include unclear success rates across different study types, handling of complex statistical methodologies, and the impact of ambiguity in natural language descriptions on reproduction accuracy.

##
keyPoints: **정보 격리 기반 재현 / Information-Isolated Reproduction**: AI 에이전트가 원본 코드와 결과를 보지 못한 상태에서 논문 방법론만으로 연구 결과를 독립적으로 재현 가능 여부를 검증하는 혁신적 접근법
**자동화된 방법론 추출 / Automated Methodology Extraction**: 논문 텍스트에서 구조화된 방법론 설명을 자동으로 추출하여 에이전트가 활용할 수 있는 형태로 변환하는 기술
**셀 단위 결과 비교 / Cell-Level Result Comparison**: 재현된 분석 결과를 원본과 결정적으로 비교하는 정교한 검증 메커니즘으로 과학적 투명성 강화
**학문적 신뢰성 자동화 / Automated Scientific Credibility**: 수작업 재현 검증을 자동화하여 사회과학 논문의 재현 가능성을 대규모로 검증하고 연구 신뢰도를 객관적으로 평가할 수 있는 기반 마련

articles.title: The Spectral Lifecycle of Transformer Training: Transient Compression Waves, Persistent Spectral Gradients, and the Q/K--V Asymmetry
articles.titleKo: 트랜스포머 훈련의 스펙트럼 생명주기: 일시적 압축파, 지속적 스펙트럼 기울기, 그리고 Q/K-V 비대칭성
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first systematic study of weight matrix singular value spectra \emph{during} transformer pretraining, tracking full SVD decompositions of every weight matrix at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M--285M parameters). We discover three phenomena: \textbf{(1)~Transient Compression Waves:} stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, creating a dramatic gradient that peaks early then \emph{reverses} -- late layers eventually over-compress past early layers. \textbf{(2)~Persistent Spectr
articles.aiSummary:
This is the first systematic study examining how weight matrix singular value spectra change during transformer pretraining. The researchers tracked full SVD decompositions of all weight matrices at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M-285M parameters). The study revealed three key phenomena: (1) Transient Compression Waves, where stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, creating a dramatic gradient that peaks early then reverses, with late layers eventually over-compressing beyond early layers. (2) Persistent Spectral Gradients that maintain consistent patterns throughout training. (3) Q/K-V Asymmetry in attention mechanisms. These findings provide crucial insights into the internal dynamics of neural network learning and could contribute to more efficient model design and training optimization in the future. However, the current analysis is limited to specific model scales.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 트랜스포머 모델의 사전학습 과정에서 가중치 행렬의 특이값 스펙트럼이 어떻게 변화하는지 체계적으로 분석한 첫 번째 연구입니다. 연구팀은 3가지 규모(3천만~2억8천5백만 개 매개변수)의 모델에서 25단계 간격으로 전체 가중치 행렬의 특이값 분해(SVD)를 추적했습니다. 연구 결과 세 가지 주요 현상을 발견했습니다: 첫째, '일시적 압축파(Transient Compression Waves)'로 초기 층에서 후기 층으로 압축이 파동처럼 전파되며 초기에 급격한 기울기를 보이다가 반전되어 후기 층이 결국 초기 층보다 과도하게 압축됩니다. 둘째, '지속적 스펙트럼 기울기'가 학습 전 과정에서 계속 유지됩니다. 셋째, Q/K-V 행렬 간 비대칭성이 관찰됩니다. 이 연구는 신경망 학습의 내부 동작 메커니즘을 이해하는 데 중요하며, 향후 더 효율적인 모델 설계와 학습 최적화에 기여할 수 있습니다. 다만 현재는 특정 규모의 모델에 한정된 분석이라는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This is the first systematic study examining how weight matrix singular value spectra change during transformer pretraining. The researchers tracked full SVD decompositions of all weight matrices at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M-285M parameters). The study revealed three key phenomena: (1) Transient Compression Waves, where stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, creating a dramatic gradient that peaks early then reverses, with late layers eventually over-compressing beyond early layers. (2) Persistent Spectral Gradients that maintain consistent patterns throughout training. (3) Q/K-V Asymmetry in attention mechanisms. These findings provide crucial insights into the internal dynamics of neural network learning and could contribute to more efficient model design and training optimization in the future. However, the current analysis is limited to specific model scales.

##
keyPoints: **일시적 압축파** / Transient Compression Waves: 안정적 순위(stable rank)의 압축이 초기층에서 후기층으로 파동처럼 전파되며, 초기에 급격한 기울기를 보이다가 반전되어 후기층이 최종적으로 초기층보다 더 압축되는 현상
**지속적 스펙트럼 기울기** / Persistent Spectral Gradients: 특이값 스펙트럼의 기울기 패턴이 전체 학습 과정 동안 일관되게 유지되는 특성
**Q/K-V 비대칭성** / Q/K-V Asymmetry: 어텐션 메커니즘의 Query/Key와 Value 행렬 간 다른 스펙트럼 특성을 보이는 현상
**다중 모델 규모 검증** / Multi-scale Validation: 3천만~2억8천5백만 개 매개변수의 다양한 모델 규모에서 일관된 패턴을 확인하여 발견의 일반화 가능성 제시

articles.title: KARL: Mitigating Hallucinations in LLMs via Knowledge-Boundary-Aware Reinforcement Learning
articles.titleKo: KARL: 지식-경계-인식 강화학습을 통한 대규모언어모델의 환각 완화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge is crucial for mitigating hallucinations. While existing reinforcement learning methods foster autonomous abstention, they often compromise answer accuracy because their static reward mechanisms, agnostic to models' knowledge boundaries, drive models toward excessive caution. In this work, we propose KARL, a novel framework that continuously aligns an LLM's abstention behavior with its evolving knowledge boundary. KARL introduces two cor
articles.aiSummary:
Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately decline questions beyond their knowledge boundaries is crucial for mitigating hallucinations and improving reliability. Existing reinforcement learning approaches promote abstention behavior but often sacrifice answer accuracy because their static reward mechanisms ignore models' actual knowledge boundaries, leading to excessive caution. This research introduces KARL, a novel framework that continuously aligns an LLM's abstention behavior with its evolving knowledge boundary through dynamic adjustments. KARL incorporates two core mechanisms that help models better distinguish between what they know and don't know. The significance of this work lies in addressing the hallucination problem while maintaining answer accuracy, a challenge previous methods struggled to balance. However, the study remains in early stages and requires extensive real-world testing before practical deployment.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
대규모 언어모델(LLM)이 자신의 지식 범위를 벗어난 질문에 답변을 거부할 수 있도록 학습하는 것이 환각(거짓 정보 생성) 문제 해결의 핵심입니다. 기존의 강화학습 방법들은 모델이 답변을 거부하도록 유도하지만, 모델의 실제 지식 범위를 고려하지 않는 고정된 보상 체계로 인해 정확한 답변 능력을 떨어뜨리는 문제가 있었습니다. 본 연구에서는 KARL이라는 새로운 프레임워크를 제안하며, 이는 LLM의 지식 경계와 그 변화에 맞춰 답변 거부 행동을 지속적으로 조정합니다. KARL은 두 가지 핵심 메커니즘을 도입하여 모델이 자신이 알고 있는 것과 모르는 것을 더 잘 구분하도록 학습시킵니다. 이 연구의 의의는 환각 문제를 완화하면서도 모델의 답변 정확도를 유지할 수 있는 방법을 제시한 것입니다. 다만 연구가 아직 초기 단계로 대규모 실제 환경 테스트가 필요한 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately decline questions beyond their knowledge boundaries is crucial for mitigating hallucinations and improving reliability. Existing reinforcement learning approaches promote abstention behavior but often sacrifice answer accuracy because their static reward mechanisms ignore models' actual knowledge boundaries, leading to excessive caution. This research introduces KARL, a novel framework that continuously aligns an LLM's abstention behavior with its evolving knowledge boundary through dynamic adjustments. KARL incorporates two core mechanisms that help models better distinguish between what they know and don't know. The significance of this work lies in addressing the hallucination problem while maintaining answer accuracy, a challenge previous methods struggled to balance. However, the study remains in early stages and requires extensive real-world testing before practical deployment.

##
keyPoints: **환각 문제의 원인과 해결 방향** / Root cause of hallucinations and solution approach: 기존 방법의 정적 보상 체계 문제를 동적 지식 경계 인식으로 해결
**KARL 프레임워크의 혁신성** / Innovation of KARL framework: 지식 경계에 맞춰 답변 거부 행동을 지속적으로 정렬하는 새로운 강화학습 방식
**성능과 신뢰성의 균형** / Balance between performance and reliability: 답변 정확도를 유지하면서 동시에 환각을 줄이는 이중 최적화
**실용화를 위한 과제** / Challenges for practical implementation: 대규모 실제 환경에서의 검증 및 성능 평가 필요
